methods.description: Techniques including Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM), Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM), and Scanning Transmission Electron Microscopy (STEM-EDS) used to determine elemental composition, sizes, and distributions of composite structures.

steps.description: The scanning electron microscopy (SEM, LEO 1430 VP, Leo Electron Microscopy Ltd., Cambridge, Great Britain) coupled with energy-dispersive X-ray detection (EDX) (XFlash 4010, Bruker AXS, Berlin, Germany) techniques were employed to determine the elemental composition, sizes, and distributions of the composite structures of the synthesized AgNPs/TiO 2 /DBP composites.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform SEM imaging and EDX analysis on composite samples to determine elemental composition, sizes, and distributions.
Perform TEM imaging on composite samples.

steps.description: ...while the STEM-EDS mode was employed for elemental analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform STEM-EDS analysis for elemental composition.
inputs: TEM instrument (Tecnai F20 X-Twin) in STEM-EDS mode

methods.description: X-ray powder diffraction method used to determine the mineral composition and investigate the formation of Ag and TiO2 nanoparticles in the composites, followed by analysis of the resulting patterns.

steps.description: The X-ray powder diffraction (XRD) method, using a Philips X'Pert Pro diffractometer (XRD, Malvern Panalytical Ltd., Malvern, UK) with Cu Kα radiation (γ = 0.1541 nm, 40 kV, 30 mA) was employed to determine the mineral composition of the obtained composites and investigate the formation of Ag and TiO 2 nanoparticles. The XRD spectrum data were collected in the angular range of 5-85 • 2θ with a step size of 0.01.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect XRD data using a diffractometer with Cu Kα radiation in the specified 2θ range.

steps.description: The X-ray spectra confirm the presence of TiO2 nanoparticles in the anatase form ( Figure 3A-C), as well as reduced metallic silver nanoparticles ( Figure 3B,C). The XRD spectrum obtained for TiO2/DBP ( Figure 3A) exhibits characteristic peaks corresponding to the anatase phase of TiO2 at 2θ angles of 25.28° and 48.01° (RRUFF ID: R070582.9). ... The spectrum displayed in Figure 3A also exhibits signals corresponding to crystalline forms of silica: quartz at 26.66 • , 39.51 • , and 60.02 • (RRUFF ID: R040031) and tridymite at 19.28 • , 21.64 • , and 32.66 • (RRUFF ID: R090042).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze XRD patterns to identify and confirm crystalline phases by matching peaks to reference databases.
inputs: Crystalline phase reference database (e.g., RRUFF ID)
outputs: Identified crystalline phases (anatase TiO2, metallic Ag, quartz, tridymite)

methods.description: Investigation of the photoluminescent properties of the composites using fluorescence spectrophotometry, followed by data processing and analysis of the resulting spectra.

steps.description: Measurements were conducted with a colloidal solution of the obtained material in water, with a concentration of 0.014 mg/mL, in a quartz cuvette with a diameter of 1.0 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare a colloidal solution of the composite material in water at a concentration of 0.014 mg/mL.

steps.description: For the investigation of the photoluminescent properties (PL) of the TiO 2 /AgNPs/DBP composites, a Hitachi F-2500 fluorescence spectrophotometer equipped with a xenon lamp was utilized. The PL spectra were recorded at room temperature using an excitation wavelength in the UV-VIS range from 200 to 750 nm.
steps.searchable_summary: Record PL spectra of the colloidal solution using a fluorescence spectrophotometer with UV-VIS excitation.
outputs: Raw PL spectra (emission intensity vs. wavelength)

steps.description: The basic spectra were corrected accordingly.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply corrections to the raw PL spectra.

steps.description: The obtained photoluminescent data were normalized using the Origin program.
In addition, data after normalization were smoothed using the Adjacent Averaging method.
steps.searchable_summary: Smooth the normalized PL data using the Adjacent Averaging method.

steps.description: Analysing the obtained photoluminescence spectra, it should be noted that four main types of photoluminescence activity (PL) can be distinguished in all materials. ... The first type of PL is associated with excitation (270 nm) and emission in the ultraviolet region (325-425 nm). ... The second type is associated with emission in the blue region of the visible spectrum (451-483 nm). ... The third type of photoluminescent activity is characterized by emission in the green region (508-524 nm). ... The final, fourth type of PL activity is associated with emissions in the red light region (630-640 nm). ... [Interpretation linking peaks to organic residue, TiO2 band transitions, self-trapped excitons, AgNPs surface plasmons, oxygen vacancies, silica defects, etc.]
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze smoothed PL spectra to identify emission peaks and interpret their origins based on material properties and literature.
inputs: Literature references on PL origins in similar materials
outputs: Interpretation of peak origins (e.g., TiO2 transitions, oxygen vacancies, AgNPs plasmons, silica defects)

methods.description: Determination of the minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) values of nanocomposite formulations against selected bacterial strains to assess antimicrobial potential.

steps.description: Table 2 and Figure 5 present the MIC values of the two different nanocomposite formulations that incorporate different silver concentrations (1.13% AgNPs/TiO 2 /DBP and 2.42% AgNPs/TiO 2 /DBP) that have been investigated against selected bacterial strains. ... The results indicate that all obtained nanocomposite formulations show antimicrobial potential against ATCC, DFI, and WI isolates exhibiting MIC values around 2.5 mg/mL against all tested strains, except for Klebsiella pneumoniae ATCC, where the MIC value was found to be one-fold lower (1.25 mg/mL).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform MIC assay on nanocomposite formulations against selected bacterial strains.
inputs: Selected bacterial strains (ATCC, DFI, WI isolates, Klebsiella pneumoniae ATCC)
Microbiological media and supplies (implied by MIC assay)
outputs: MIC values for each formulation against each bacterial strain
